rationale: The supervisor uses a wall-following systematic coverage strategy, introducing a 'turning' helper state to avoid oscillations and ensure safe reactions. All uncontrollable events are handled in every state. Forward movement is only allowed when the path is clear. When obstacles are detected, the robot rotates to avoid them, and 'turning' helps prevent immediate reversal. This encourages full area coverage while preventing collisions and redundant movement.
coverage_strategy: Prioritize moving forward when the path is clear.
Rotate (preferably counterclockwise) to follow walls and avoid obstacles.
Use a 'turning' state to prevent immediate reversal and encourage systematic wall-following.
When stuck (obstacles on multiple sides), allow full rotation to find a new clear path.
Never allow move_forward or random walk when an obstacle is detected ahead.
Encourage systematic, non-oscillatory coverage by biasing turns in one direction.
